page: Microsoft Azure D2nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core and 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 1638.19 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 26.2 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance are limited. The stress-ng multi-core test scored 1679.98, and memory bandwidth tests show low throughput, with large block read and write speeds at 15.54 GB/sec and 11.76 GB/sec respectively. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also on the lower end, with Redis achieving 2.28 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at 3.59 GB/sec.

Given its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and lack of local storage, the D2nls_v6 offers a specialized hardware profile designed for network-heavy tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high clock speeds and advanced vector or matrix instructions on a budget, without the overhead of unused RAM. Suitable workloads include lightweight network appliances, basic web serving, single-threaded task processing, and small-scale utility services.
description: Microsoft Azure D2nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 2 vCPUs from a single physical core, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. The processor features a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth results. With its 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is a qualitatively cost-effective option for network-focused workloads, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded applications that do not demand extensive memory or local disk space.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight, network-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processing and a low-memory footprint for specialized single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
2 vCPUs with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
